title: Sweet Smoky Filipino Gruyere Fritters
description: A fermented and fluffy Filipino dish featuring Gruyere cheese, deep-fried to perfection, and suitable for diabetic-friendly diets.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, yeast, salt, sugar, and smoked paprika.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and shredded Gruyere cheese.
Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Knead the batter for 5 minutes until it becomes elastic and fluffy.
Let the batter rest in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Using a spoon, drop small balls of the batter into the hot oil, about 1/4 cup each.
Fry the fritters for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Remove the fritters from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the Sweet Smoky Filipino Gruyere Fritters warm and enjoy.
